slides.headline: Expanding Down Under
slides.subheadline: Market opportunities, strategy, and implementation plan — Advay Kumar

slides.headline: Executive Summary
bullets: Objective: profitable footprint within 18–24 months.
Opportunity: resilient, high‑income economy, strong consumer power.
Alignment: supports global growth, APAC diversification goals.
Capital & ROI: staged investment; break‑even by month 24–30.

slides.headline: Market Overview
bullets: Economy: AAA stability, strong Asia‑Pacific trade ties.
Demographics: 26M, urban hubs Sydney, Melbourne, Brisbane.
Behavior: digital natives expect seamless, high‑touch service.
Sector trends: add two sector growth trends.

slides.headline: Competitive Landscape
bullets: Incumbents: leading local and global competitors.
Market gaps: underserved segments, experience pain points.
Our edge: differentiated product, data, service quality.
Barriers: local loyalty and high operating costs.

slides.headline: Regulatory & Legal
bullets: Corporate: ASIC registration and governance requirements.
Taxation: corporate, GST, PAYG obligations.
Employment: Fair Work, NES, superannuation mandates.
Privacy: comply with Australian Privacy Principles.

left.title: Entry model
bullets: Recommend subsidiary with strong local leadership.
Evaluate JV or distribution partnerships.
Pilot in one state before scaling.
Phase rollout based on demand signals.

right.title: Localization & logistics
bullets: Tailor brand voice to Australian nuance.
Show pricing clearly with taxes.
Optimize fulfillment across vast geography.
Support regional service hours.

left.title: Technology
bullets: Localize e‑commerce, CRM, and service channels.
Secure payments: BPAY and direct entry.
Local data hosting where required.

right.title: People & governance
bullets: Hire local leadership and specialists.
Build frontline support teams.
Define reporting to global headquarters.

left.title: Investment & revenue
bullets: Initial setup, licensing, and marketing budgets.
Revenue forecast months 12, 24, 36.
OpEx: wages, real estate, platforms.

right.title: Risk mitigation
bullets: FX buffers and hedging policies.
Contingency for demand volatility.
Scenario planning and burn limits.

slides.headline: Implementation Timeline
bullets: Phase 1 (1–3): legal, compliance, early hires.
Phase 2 (4–6): infrastructure, localization, soft launch.
Phase 3 (7–12): full launch, marketing, review.
Next steps: approvals, budget, due diligence.

slides.headline: Ready to expand in Australia
bullets: Call to action: request board approval to proceed.
Contact: Advay Kumar.
